tree.setup: You're eating at a restaurant with a friend. You're allergic to peanuts and prefer not to eat very spicy food. You need to communicate your dietary restrictions and customize your order.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友在一家餐厅吃饭。你对花生过敏，而且不太能吃辣。你需要跟服务员说清楚你的饮食限制，定制你的菜。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两位好，这是菜单。我们的特色菜是宫保鸡丁和麻辣豆腐，要不要试试？
turns.text_pinyin: liǎng wèi hǎo, zhè shì càidān. wǒmen de tèsè cài shì gōngbǎo jīdīng hé málà dòufu, yào bú yào shìshi?
turns.text_en: Hello you two, here's the menu. Our specialties are Kung Pao Chicken and Mapo Tofu -- would you like to try them?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain your dietary restrictions and ask about the dishes.

options.text_zh: 谢谢。不好意思，我得先说一下，我对花生过敏，所以宫保鸡丁我不能吃。另外，我不太能吃辣。你们的麻辣豆腐能做不辣的吗？或者你能推荐几个不辣、不含花生的菜吗？
options.text_pinyin: xièxie. bùhǎoyìsi, wǒ děi xiān shuō yīxià, wǒ duì huāshēng guòmǐn, suǒyǐ gōngbǎo jīdīng wǒ bù néng chī. lìngwài, wǒ bú tài néng chī là. nǐmen de málà dòufu néng zuò bú là de ma? huòzhě nǐ néng tuījiàn jǐ gè bú là, bù hán huāshēng de cài ma?
options.text_en: Thanks. Sorry, I need to mention first that I'm allergic to peanuts, so I can't eat the Kung Pao Chicken. Also, I can't really handle spicy food. Can your Mapo Tofu be made non-spicy? Or could you recommend some dishes that are not spicy and don't contain peanuts?
options.feedback: Clear and thorough -- states the allergy upfront, explains both restrictions, and asks for alternatives. Using 过敏 correctly is important for food safety.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没问题，过敏的话一定要注意。宫保鸡丁里面有花生，确实不能吃。我推荐西红柿炒鸡蛋、清蒸鱼，还有蒜蓉西兰花，这几个都不辣也没有花生。
turns.text_pinyin: méi wèntí, guòmǐn dehuà yīdìng yào zhùyì. gōngbǎo jīdīng lǐmiàn yǒu huāshēng, quèshí bù néng chī. wǒ tuījiàn xīhóngshì chǎo jīdàn, qīngzhēng yú, hái yǒu suànróng xīlánhuā, zhè jǐ gè dōu bú là yě méiyǒu huāshēng.
turns.text_en: No problem, allergies are definitely something to be careful about. Kung Pao Chicken does have peanuts, so you definitely can't eat that. I recommend tomato scrambled eggs, steamed fish, and broccoli with garlic -- none of these are spicy or contain peanuts.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Order your dishes and confirm the details.

options.text_zh: 听起来都不错。那我们要一个清蒸鱼和一个蒜蓉西兰花。我朋友想吃辣的，再来一个麻辣豆腐。对了，清蒸鱼里面不会放花生吧？我想再确认一下。
options.text_pinyin: tīng qǐlái dōu búcuò. nà wǒmen yào yī gè qīngzhēng yú hé yī gè suànróng xīlánhuā. wǒ péngyou xiǎng chī là de, zài lái yī gè málà dòufu. duìle, qīngzhēng yú lǐmiàn bú huì fàng huāshēng ba? wǒ xiǎng zài quèrèn yīxià.
options.text_en: Those all sound good. We'll have one steamed fish and one broccoli with garlic. My friend wants something spicy, so add one Mapo Tofu. Oh, the steamed fish won't have peanuts, right? I just want to double-check.
options.feedback: Orders for both people, accommodates the friend's preferences too, and double-checks for the allergen. This level of care with a food allergy is exactly right.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 放心，清蒸鱼就是鱼加一点姜和葱，绝对没有花生。三个菜加米饭，还需要别的吗？喝点什么？
turns.text_pinyin: fàngxīn, qīngzhēng yú jiùshì yú jiā yīdiǎn jiāng hé cōng, juéduì méiyǒu huāshēng. sān gè cài jiā mǐfàn, hái xūyào bié de ma? hē diǎn shénme?
turns.text_en: Don't worry, the steamed fish is just fish with a bit of ginger and scallion, absolutely no peanuts. Three dishes and rice -- do you need anything else? Something to drink?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Finish ordering and thank the server.

options.text_zh: 这个问题很复杂，我需要更多时间考虑。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège wèntí hěn fùzá, wǒ xūyào gèng duō shíjiān kǎolǜ.
options.text_en: This question is very complex; I need more time to consider it.
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with the specific question at hand.

options.text_zh: 好的，那再来一壶茶吧。非常感谢你帮我注意过敏的问题，这样我吃得放心多了。
options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, nà zài lái yī hú chá ba. fēicháng gǎnxiè nǐ bāng wǒ zhùyì guòmǐn de wèntí, zhèyàng wǒ chī de fàngxīn duō le.
options.text_en: OK, and a pot of tea please. Thank you so much for being careful about my allergy -- it makes me feel much more at ease eating.
options.feedback: Gracious -- thanking the server specifically for accommodating your allergy encourages attentive service and leaves a warm impression.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这不是最重要的问题。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhè bú shì zuì zhòngyào de wèntí.
options.text_en: I don't think this is the most important question.
options.feedback: Redirects rather than addressing the topic directly.

tree.cultural_note: Food allergies (食物过敏 shíwù guòmǐn) are taken increasingly seriously in Chinese restaurants, but it's always wise to state your allergy clearly and double-check. Peanuts (花生 huāshēng) and peanut oil (花生油 huāshēng yóu) are common in Chinese cooking. When ordering for a group, balancing everyone's preferences is considered thoughtful. Ordering a pot of tea (一壶茶 yī hú chá) to share is a standard part of a Chinese meal.
